brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Same query as before. But name the engagements table 'e' and the clients table 'c'. Use those aliases everywhere. SQL was designed by people who hated typing.

expected.sql: SELECT e.era, e.year, c.name FROM chrono_engagements e INNER JOIN chrono_clients c ON e.client_id = c.client_id LIMIT 10

hints.text: Carol: 'Once an alias is defined, the long table name doesn''t work anymore. e.era, c.name, etc.'
Carol: 'e for engagements. c for clients. Use them everywhere.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: Carol nods. 'Better. You'll do this a lot. Get comfortable with it.'
